title: Fainting
summary: Fainting (syncope) is a temporary loss of consciousness due to reduced blood flow to the brain. Most cases recover quickly.
steps: If the person feels faint: have them sit or lie down immediately to prevent injury from a fall.
If they have fainted: lay the person flat on their back in a safe location.
Raise their legs 15–30 cm (about 12 inches) above the level of their heart.
Loosen any tight clothing around the neck, chest, and waist.
Ensure fresh air — open windows or move the person to a ventilated area.
Check for normal breathing and pulse.
When conscious, keep the person lying down for a few more minutes.
Offer water slowly after full recovery — do not give if still unconscious.
what_to_do: Lay the person flat and raise their legs
Let the person rest before standing up
what_not_to_do: Do NOT leave the person sitting in a chair — lay them flat
Do NOT splash cold water on the face forcefully
Do NOT give food or drink until fully conscious
Do NOT allow the person to stand up too quickly after recovering
Do NOT crowd around the person — give them space and air
when_to_call_ambulance: The person does not regain consciousness within 1–2 minutes
The person had a seizure or convulsions
Fainting occurred after a head injury or during exercise
Person is diabetic, elderly, or has a heart condition
Breathing is absent or irregular after fainting
Fainting is accompanied by chest pain or palpitations
